methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $26M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Terraform Industries is a Series A-stage climate and energy company that has raised $26M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (LNZA), with direct peer data from three same-sector, same-stage companies: Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the $26M raised proxy, implying a $52M valuation. The peer-median method uses the median valuation of the three climate and energy peers at Series A stage, yielding $150M; the peer range spans $110M to $200M. These methods generate meaningfully different signals, reflecting uncertainty around whether Terraform commands a premium or discount relative to observed peer valuations.

The triangulated analysis across both methods supports a range of $44M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $101M. The wide dispersion—a 3.9x spread from low to high—reflects tension between stage-based progression on limited data versus peer comparables with greater absolute valuations. Key uncertainties include relative competitive positioning within the peer set, capital efficiency relative to peers' burn profiles, and whether the peer set adequately reflects Terraform's specific technical or market risk profile. Additional clarity on near-term milestones and burn trajectory would meaningfully narrow this range.
